message:
style(fr-conformance): rustfmt reflow of live_oracle_audit fn-call wrapping

Pure rustfmt pass on the
`live_oracle_audit_requires_matrix_coverage_or_exemptions_for_fixture_and_workflow_gaps`
test body: the two `live_oracle_audit_exemption_map(..., ...)` calls
(one for `fixture_exemptions` / `fixture`, one for
`planned_differential_hook_exemptions` / `journey_id`) now collapse
onto a single line where they fit under rustfmt's line-length budget,
and onto a two-line "fn name + arguments-on-next-line" form where
they do not. No predicate logic moves; the audit still consumes the
same exemption map structure committed in the schema introduction.

Running `cargo fmt --check -p fr-conformance` now reports no drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
